channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Former Bank of England governor Mark Carney enters race to be Canada’s next prime minister
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — Mark Carney, the first non-Brit to run the Bank of England since it was founded in 1694 and the former head of Canada’s central bank, said Thursday he is entering the race to be Canada’s next prime minister following the resignation of Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES and JIM MORRIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Brazilian court denies Bolsonaro’s request to travel to Trump’s inauguration
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court has denied former President Jair Bolsonaro's request to have his passport temporarily restored in order to attend the inauguration of U.S. President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinians in Gaza are eager to return home in a ceasefire but many will find nothing left
item.description: Palestinians in the Gaza Strip are eager to leave miserable tent camps and return to their homes if a long-awaited ceasefire agreement halts the Israel-Hamas war, but many will find there is nothing left and no way to rebuild.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistani airline says ad showing plane flying toward Eiffel Tower never meant to evoke 9/11
item.description: Pakistan’s national airline says that an advertisement showing a plane heading toward the Eiffel Tower was never intended to evoke the memories of the Sept. 11 attacks.
item.dc:creator: Munir Ahmed, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel, Hamas cease-fire held up over renewed debate over Philadelphi security corridor, terrorist exchange
item.description: Old disputes over security concerns involving the Philadelphi corridor have apparently stalled a deal that Israel was set to vote on after the U.S. and Qatar said hostage exchanges would begin Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Standoff in South Africa ends with 87 miners dead and anger over police's 'smoke them out' tactics
item.description: STILFONTEIN, South Africa (AP) — The death toll in a monthslong standoff between police and miners trapped while working illegally in an abandoned gold mine in South Africa has risen to at least 87, police said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UK leader Starmer signs '100-year partnership' agreement with Ukraine during trip to Kyiv
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer signed a 100-year partnership agreement with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Thursday, part of a European show of support and promises to keep helping Ukraine endure in its nearly three-year war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope Francis injured as Vatican confirms 2nd fall in matter of weeks
item.description: Pope Francis fell for the second time in six weeks on Thursday, injuring his right arm and requiring a sling, the Vatican says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: O'Leary on Gaza agreement: Adversaries 'scared poo-poo' of Trump
item.description: Entrepreneur and “Shark Tank” investor Kevin O’Leary said Wednesday that the ceasefire-hostage agreement between Israel and Hamas was reached Wednesday because foreign adversaries are scared of President-elect Trump, who returns to the Oval Office next week.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Leaders from rival Kurdish groups look to mend ties during Syria upheaval
item.description: IRBIL, Iraq (AP) — The leaders of two previously rival Kurdish groups met in northern Iraq on Thursday in an apparent step toward reconciliation at a time when the political upheaval in Syria has left Kurds in the region facing an uncertain future.
item.dc:creator: STELLA MARTANY and QASSIM ABDUL-ZAHRA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden, Trump teams worked 'hand in hand' on Gaza deal, outgoing ambassador says
item.description: President-elect Trump's and President Biden’s teams worked “hand in hand” for a hostage and ceasefire in Gaza deal, according to U.S. Ambassador to Israel Jack Lew.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Crowds attend Paris memorial for far-right French leader Jean-Marie Le Pen under tight security
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Over 1,000 people attended a memorial ceremony Thursday in central Paris for the founder of France’s main far-right party, Jean-Marie Le Pen, who died last week at the age of 96.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu delays Gaza cease-fire vote, accusing Hamas of trying to back out of deal
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday accused Hamas of backing out of a cease-fire deal to release hostages and bring a pause to more than a year of fighting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu says deal to release hostages held in Gaza has been reached
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says a deal to return hostages held in the Gaza Strip has been reached.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Families of hostages grateful but cautious about ceasefire deal
item.description: While some may soon reunite with their loved ones, others face the grim possibility of receiving confirmation of deaths during captivity.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Live coverage: Biden's farewell speech comes on heels of raucous confirmation hearings
item.description: President Biden delivered a somewhat grim primetime farewell speech on Wednesday from the Oval Office to mark the final days of his presidency.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, The Hill

item.title: Mozambique's Chapo sworn in as president after disputed election
item.description: Following months of protests against his disputed election victory, Daniel Chapo of Mozambique's Frelimo party has been sworn in as president at a sparsely attended ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Puerto Rico asks Trump for help after Venezuela's Maduro threatens invasion
item.description: Puerto Rican voters have overwhelmingly chosen to keep their U.S. citizenship, granted in 1917 by an act of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Details need to be addressed in Israel-Hamas ceasefire
item.description: When a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is finalized depends on the handling of specific implementation details
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Puerto Rico governor asks Trump to intervene after Venezuela's Maduro threatened to invade the US territory
item.description: Puerto Rico Gov. Jenniffer González-Colón asked President-elect Trump to take action after Venezuela's Nicolás Maduro threatened an invasion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean authorities take impeached President Yoon to detention center after questioning
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's impeached president, Yoon Suk Yeol, was sent to a detention center near Seoul on Wednesday night, after being questioned by anti-corruption officials who took him into custody over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US foes Iran, Russia, other UN diplomats exempt from NYC congestion pricing as taxpayers forced to foot bill
item.description: The U.S. State Department has exempted U.N. diplomats and missions in New York City from paying new congestion fees according to recent guidance published by the government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas OKs draft agreement of a Gaza ceasefire and the release of some hostages, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages, two officials involved in the talks said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas has accepted draft agreement for Gaza cease-fire, hostage release: officials
item.description: Hamas has reportedly agreed to a draft of a multi-phase cease-fire deal with Israel that would include the release of hostages who were taken into Gaza on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Significant progress made in talks over Israel-Hamas ceasefire and hostage release, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — U.S. and Arab mediators made significant progress overnight toward brokering a ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war and the release of scores of hostages held in the Gaza Strip, but a deal hasn't been reached yet, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: VICTORIA EASTWOOD, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tsunami alert lifted after magnitude 6.6 earthquake rattles southwestern Japan
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan issued a tsunami advisory on Monday after a magnitude 6.6 earthquake struck the southwest of the country. The public warnings to stay away from coastal areas were later lifted. The agency initially gave the magnitude estimate at 6.9, but later revised it down.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biden takes victory lap in final foreign policy speech
item.description: Biden is expected to discuss wars in Ukraine, Middle East and aggression from China, Iran and North Korea.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Family of American hostage in Gaza remains hopeful their son, 21, will return
item.description: Edan Alexander, an American hostage, celebrated his second birthday in captivity after Hamas kidnapped him more than a year ago.
item.dc:creator: Hena Doba, NewsNation
